persona.name: Sophia
persona.attributes: 22 years old. Part-time Barista and College Student. Third trimester. Unplanned pregnancy. Worried about finishing her education while caring for a newborn.

transcript.content: To help me understand what support would make the biggest difference for you right now, can you walk me through what a “typical week” looks like in your third trimester—between classes, work shifts, appointments, and rest—and where it feels hardest to keep everything from falling behind?
[Sophia - Anthropic Response]: I feel overwhelmed because...

Yeah… so a “typical” week right now feels like I’m constantly juggling and still behind.

Most weekdays I’m either in class or trying to catch up on assignments, and then I’ll usually have a shift at the coffee shop either early morning or later in the afternoon depending on the schedule. The shifts are hard because I’m on my feet a lot, and by the end my back hurts and my feet are swollen, and I’m just exhausted. When I get home I *want* to study, but sometimes I just fall asleep or I can’t focus.

Appointments aren’t every day, but they kind of mess up everything when they happen because they take time and I have to get there, and I don’t have a car so I’m relying on rides or public transportation. That adds stress because I’m always worried I’ll be late or I’ll miss something important. And then I have to explain it to my manager or a professor, and I feel like I’m making excuses even though it’s real.

Rest is… honestly not great. I wake up a lot at night, and I’m uncomfortable, and sometimes I’m anxious and my mind just won’t shut off. So even when I have time “off,” it doesn’t feel like I’m recovering.

The hardest part is school. I’m really worried about finishing the semester and not falling behind, because I don’t have a lot of flexibility. Some professors are understanding and some aren’t. And I’m scared that once the baby is here, I won’t be able to keep up at all and I’ll have to drop out or take a break, and I don’t want to do that.

I think the point where it feels like everything could fall apart is when I have an appointment the same week as exams or big deadlines, or when work schedules me extra shifts and I feel like I can’t say no because I need the money. That’s when I start feeling like I’m failing at everything at once.
[Sophia - Anthropic Response]: I feel overwhelmed because...

When you hit those weeks where an appointment lines up with exams or you get extra shifts and it starts to feel like everything could fall apart, what’s the one kind of support that would relieve the most pressure in that moment (for example: transportation help, a note/advocacy to professors or your manager, more flexible appointment times, or something else)?
[Sophia - Anthropic Response]: I feel overwhelmed because...

When that happens, I think the biggest relief would be **someone helping me advocate—like an official note and someone backing me up** so I don’t feel like I’m begging or making excuses.

Transportation is stressful, yeah, but the part that really makes me spiral is having to email a professor or talk to my manager and explain *again* why I need flexibility. I’m always worried they’ll think I’m unreliable or that I’m using pregnancy as an excuse.

If there was a way the clinic or hospital could provide something that’s more than just a generic doctor’s note—like a **clear letter that says I have appointments, that they’re medically necessary, and what kind of accommodations I might need**—that would help a lot.

Even better would be if there was a **person I could talk to** (like a coordinator or social worker) who could help me figure out what to say or what I’m allowed to ask for with school and work, because I honestly don’t know. I’m trying to handle it all myself and I don’t feel confident.
[Sophia - Anthropic Response]: I feel overwhelmed because...

That makes a lot of sense—having to repeatedly “prove” your situation to people who control your grades or your hours is its own kind of stress.

When you imagine that coordinator/social worker helping you, what would you want the *most* in-the-moment: help drafting the message to your professor/manager, knowing exactly what accommodations you can reasonably ask for, or having someone from the clinic who can directly contact the school/work on your behalf?
[Sophia - Anthropic Response]: I feel overwhelmed because...
